name: Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum)
title: Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum) | The Hippie Scientist
description: Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum) is a medicinal mushroom used across East Asian traditions for immune regulation and adaptogenic support. Its beta-glucan polysaccharides and triterpenes (ganoderic acids) modulate innate and adaptive immunity, NK cell activity, and inflammatory signaling. Clinical evidence supports immunomodulatory effects; sleep and fatigue data are emerging.

title: Schisandra (Schisandra chinensis) | The Hippie Scientist
description: Schisandra (Schisandra chinensis) is a five-flavor berry used in TCM as an adaptogen and liver tonic. Its primary lignans (schisandrin A/B/C) support hepatocyte detoxification via CYP450 modulation and exhibit antioxidant activity. Human data support mild stress reduction and liver enzyme normalization in hepatitis populations.

name: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum)
title: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) | The Hippie Scientist
description: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.

name: Supports nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis, cognitive function, and mild mood regulation. Promotes neurogenesis and neuroprotection in preclinical models. Human evidence is moderate and emerging, with the strongest signals for mild cognitive support and emotional well-being in aging or stressed adults. Effects are generally subtle and cumulative rather than acute.
title: Supports nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis, cognitive function, and mild mood regulation. Promotes neurogenesis and neuroprotection in preclinical models. name: Turkey Tail
title: Turkey Tail | The Hippie Scientist
description: Turkey Tail (Trametes versicolor) is a shelf mushroom yielding two extensively studied polysaccharopeptides: PSK (krestin) and PSP. These compounds stimulate NK cells, macrophages, and T-cell activity, and PSK is approved in Japan as an adjunct cancer immunotherapy. Human evidence for standalone immune support in healthy populations is limited but supportive.

sources.title: Curcumin: A Review of Its Effects on Human Health (Hewlings & Kalman, 2017)
Efficacy of Turmeric Extracts and Curcumin for Alleviating Symptoms of Joint Arthritis (Daily et al., 2016)
